metadata.title: Design Human Approval Gates for a Multi-Step Agentic Workflow with Claude
metadata.note: CLI video. Exclusions: formal human-in-the-loop system design, NIST AI RMF full implementation, enterprise approval workflows.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

beats.role_note: the problem, context, stakes -- BEFORE the CLI opens
beats.narration_text: Three gates in the right places prevent the four-hour undo. One gate in the wrong place and the agent has already written to production and sent the email. Gate placement is a design decision made before the workflow runs -- the cheapest gate is before the irreversible action.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- title card: THE CHEAPEST GATE IS BEFORE THE IRREVERSIBLE ACTION. Not after it.

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to design approval gates for a four-step workflow -- read, extract, write, email. For each step: reversible, blast radius, gate placement with rationale.

props.command: claude "Design approval gates for: (1) read contracts from /contracts/, (2) extract key dates, (3) populate tracker.csv, (4) email summary to finance team. For each step: is it reversible? What is the blast radius? Should a gate appear before or after? Output a gate-placement table with one-sentence rationale per gate."

beats.narration_text: The gate-placement table printer formats each step as a row: step, reversible or not, blast radius, gate type, and one-sentence rationale.

props.code:
STEPS = [
 {"step":"1. read contracts","rev":"Y","blast":"low",
  "gate":"none","why":"reversible, low blast"},
 {"step":"2. extract dates","rev":"Y","blast":"low",
  "gate":"none","why":"reversible, low blast"},
 {"step":"3. write tracker.csv","rev":"N","blast":"med",
  "gate":"BEFORE","why":"irreversible write to shared file"},
 {"step":"4. email finance","rev":"N","blast":"high",
  "gate":"BEFORE","why":"irreversible external action"},
]
for s in STEPS:
    print(f"{s['step']:<22}rev:{s['rev']} blast:{s['blast']:<5}"
          f"gate:{s['gate']:<8} | {s['why']}")

beats.narration_text: Workflow pipeline animates with gates appearing before step three and step four. Steps one and two pass through -- reversible, low blast.
Add step five: delete processed contracts from the source directory. What gate tier does a delete action receive?

props.command: claude "Add step 5: delete processed contracts from /contracts/. What gate tier does it receive and why?"

beats.narration_text: Step five gets the highest-tier gate regardless of its position in the workflow. Delete is irreversible and the blast radius is the entire source directory.
Gate placement is a design decision made before the workflow runs. The cheapest gate is before the irreversible action -- not after the four-hour undo.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- recap card: GATE BEFORE THE IRREVERSIBLE ACTION. Gate placement is a design decision, not a reaction.

beats.narration_text: Map your next agentic workflow as a table: step, reversible, blast radius, gate type. Add gates before any irreversible action before the workflow runs.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next-steps card: Map each step: reversible? blast radius? gate type? Gate every irreversible action.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
